page: Amazon Web Services c6g.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the custom AWS Graviton2 processor. It features 48 dedicated physical ARMv8 cores based on the Neoverse-N1 architecture, running at 2.5 GHz. The system provides 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. The instance achieves strong multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, alongside strong static web serving throughput. However, single-core metrics, such as Geekbench single-core scores and single-threaded compression tasks, fall into the weak category. Memory bandwidth remains average across both small cached and large uncached block reads. Large-model LLM inference shows poor performance for prompt processing on 70B models, though text generation speeds for 7B and 70B models remain average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture delivers high resource density and strong multi-core throughput relative to its hardware profile, making it a viable alternative to x86 instances for parallelizable workloads. The lack of hyperthreading ensures consistent execution times. This instance is suited for multi-threaded workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and multi-threaded decompression tasks, whereas single-threaded applications or memory-intensive databases may experience performance bottlenecks.
description: Amazon Web Services c6g.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server instance utilizing the AWS Graviton2 processor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores on the Neoverse-N1 architecture running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 96.0 GB of memory and a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU capabilities and high static web serving throughput, contrasted with weaker single-core and single-threaded compression performance. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated core allocation provide qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized workloads. This server is suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations that leverage ARM architecture.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6g.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server with 48 dedicated Graviton2 cores, 96 GB RAM, and 20 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core performance.

tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring forty-eight dedicated physical cores and balanced multi-core performance for parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton2 ARM64 physical cores at 2.5 GHz.
Equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory and 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Delivers strong multi-core CPU performance and high static web serving throughput.
Exhibits lower single-core and single-threaded compression performance.
Suited for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and database operations.
